Week of 11/17 - 11/28

Weekly Market Summary

By Noah Halfon

Global Weekly Market Movements

U.S. Equities

  • S&P 500 Index: 6,849.09 (4.47%)
    • SPX rebounds to gain back 75% of losses, the best reversal ever in November as renewed hopes of rate cuts and labor data came forth. Every sector finished green for the week led by XLY consumers at 4.81% and XLK technology at 4.71%. As investors “buy the dip,” last week's average daily notional SPX option volume hits a record high $3.5 trillion with 95 million contracts traded.
  • NASDAQ Composite: 23,365.69 (5.43%)
  • Dow Jones Industrial Average: 47,716 (4.16)
  • Russell 3000 Index: 3,888.11 (4.77%)

Big Movers of the Week

    • Robinhood (HOOD): 128.49 (20.98%)
      • Shares of Robinhood soared this week after announcing plans to expand its prediction markets business through a new futures and derivatives exchanges. The company has already done over $9 billion of contracts within a year of launch. Coupled with expectations for Fed easing, rate cuts will lower margin costs and increase brokerage activity.
    • Intel (INTC): 40.56 (20.97%)
      • Intel stock pops on Friday after rumors of it becoming a new chip producer for Apple's M‑series chips starting around 2027. While rumors are unconfirmed, this may represent an amazing opportunity for Intel's foundry business and a diversification for Apple who relies heavily on TSMC.
    • Alphabet (GOOG): 320.12 (12.30%)
      • Google hits new all time highs after signing a multi million dollar deal with NATO for cloud computing and news that META is considering Google TPU’s (tensor processing units; chips for AI/ML) for their data centers in a deal worth billions. Also bullish, Google’s flagship AI model Gemini 3 came out (trained on custom Google TPU’s) and has been remarked as one of the best models out there with OpenAI commenting on tailwinds as well. Will Google begin to take Nvidia market share?

Chinese Equities - Shanghai Composite (SHCOMP): 3,905.58 (1.43%)

Hong Kong Equities - Hang Seng Index (HSI): 25,858.89 (1.60%)

Japanese Equities- Nikkei 225 (NI225): 50,253.91 (2.04%)

  • Ethereum (ETH): 2,829.23 (-6.55%)

(SEE DOWN BELOW ON RISK ON ASSET IMPLICATIONS (IE. CRYPTO LIQUIDATIONS))

U.S. Monetary & Fiscal Policy

CME Fedwatch: Stocks rallied heavily as expectations for a December rate cut hit over 80% following both NYC Fed President John Williams and San Francisco President Mary Daly signaling support. Williams is often viewed as a proxy for Powell, helping push market odds from 30% to 75% in a single day. Markets are more optimistic about an end of year cut, which underpins the recent rally among weakening labor data releases. Coming soon will be the announcement for the next Fed Chair, possibly highlighting the emergence of a shadow Fed who makes Powell’s views irrelevant.

Global Macroeconomic News

Jobless Claims/Private Payrolls: The US jobless claims report 216,000 vs the 235,000 expected, but the unemployment rate stays at a four year high of 4.4%. US Private Payrolls also fell by an average of -13,500 per week in the 4 weeks ending November 8th. These reports suggest a labor market that is cooling through reduced demand for workers rather than just rising layoffs, where slower wage growth and softer consumer spending contribute to weakening sentiment. This may be all some Fed officials need to justify cuts, as all eyes will be on next week's PCE report.

Japan Bonds & Yen Arbitrage: Japanese bond yields continue to rise after the announcement of Japan's ¥21.3 trillion ($135B) stimulus package. The Japanese 10 year yield hit 1.83%, the highest level since April 2008, as investors sell amidst worries of inflation, credit trustworthiness, and uncertain monetary policy actions. This matters because Japan is one of the largest owners of US treasuries, and caution in their fixed income and debt markets means that Japan may have to finance with a higher interest rate. Because of this, fears of Japan selling their $1.1 trillion holdings of US bonds to be able to finance themselves could harm US bond markets and implicate interest rates.

There is also the Yen carry trade, where hedge funds and traders historically short the Yen and go long US assets or tech stocks. Japan's commitment to a depreciating national currency against the dollar and their low rates result in a large Yen and US dollar FX spread. Now, since the Yen is appreciating because of increased stimulus and because of rate hikes, lots of long positions that are short on the Yen and long US assets now have to liquidated to cover those short positions. Because of this, many risk on assets (like Bitcoin for example) have been seeing price selloffs and changes in investor sentiment around risk.

JPY/USD Spread (divergence)

JGB 2 Year

U.S. Consumer Confidence: Consumer confidence hits an all time low, as The Conference Board’s gauge slid in November amid growing anxiety about rising unemployment and inflation. University of Michigan consumer confidence survey also showed that the November sentiment index dropped to 51 from 53.6. Weaker sentiment could slow consumer spending, a key engine of growth, which may reinforce the Fed’s view that the economy is still slowing even during the holiday spending season.

NVIDIA Earnings Recap: The most valuable company in the world reported: $57B revenue (+62% YoY), $1.30 EPS (+60%) with data center revenue hitting $51.2B (+66%), driving 73.4% gross margins and Q4 revenue projections of $63.7 - $66.3B vs. $61.98B estimates. CEO Jensen Huang also commented that Blackwell GPU sales are “off the charts” and cloud GPUs are sold out. Highlighting the surging AI compute demand and rapid expansion of the global AI ecosystem facilitated by Nvidia, the company continues to deliver. After earnings, the US also approved 70,000 Blackwell AI chips to Saudi Arabia, UAE. Analysts across the street upped price targets following Q3 earnings.

Sources: Google Finance, Market Watch, CME Fedwatch, Yahoo Finance, Reuters, New York Times, Bloomberg, Wall St Journal, Washington Post, US Department of the Treasury, GuruFocus, Edward Jones

Noah Halfon
